mission.title: Install and Configure Kind (Kubernetes in Docker)
mission.description: Kind (Kubernetes in Docker) is a tool for running local Kubernetes clusters using Docker container nodes. It is primarily designed for testing Kubernetes itself but can also be used for local development or CI. The key trade-off is that it requires Docker to be installed and may not be suitable for production environments.

steps.title: Install Docker
steps.description:
Kind requires Docker to create and manage Kubernetes clusters. Ensure Docker is installed and running on your machine.

```bash

sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y docker.io

brew install --cask docker

```

> For Ubuntu/Debian systems.
> For macOS users.
commands: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y docker.io

steps.title: Download and Install Kind
steps.description:
This step installs Kind by downloading the binary for your platform and placing it in your PATH.

```bash

[ $(uname -m) = x86_64 ] && curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-$(uname)-amd64 && chmod +x ./kind && sudo mv ./kind /usr/local/bin/kind

[ $(uname -m) = aarch64 ] && curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-$(uname)-arm64 && chmod +x ./kind && sudo mv ./kind /usr/local/bin/kind

```

> For AMD64 / x86_64 on Linux.
> For ARM64 on Linux.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a local Kubernetes cluster using Kind with the default configuration.

```bash

kind create cluster --image kindest/node:v1.31.0

```

> This creates a cluster with Kubernetes version 1.31.

steps.title: Verify the Cluster
steps.description:
Check that the cluster is up and running by listing the nodes.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with their status.

uninstall.title: Remove the Kind Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the Kind cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that any data stored in the cluster will be lost.

```bash

kind delete cluster

```

> This will remove the cluster and all its resources.

uninstall.title: Remove Kind Binary
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Kind binary from your system.

```bash

sudo rm /usr/local/bin/kind

```

> This will delete the Kind executable.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kind
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of any important data. Then, upgrade Kind to the latest version.

```bash

curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-$(uname)-amd64 && chmod +x ./kind && sudo mv ./kind /usr/local/bin/kind

kind version

```

> This command downloads the latest Kind binary.
> Verify the upgrade by checking the Kind version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kind: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kind binary is not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the directory containing the Kind binary is added to your PATH.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster: failed to start nodes'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Docker may not be running or there are insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Start Docker and ensure you have enough resources allocated (minimum 2 vCPU, 4GB RAM).

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster may not be running.

**Fix:**
Run 'kind get clusters' to check the status of your clusters.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Image not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Kubernetes version image is not available.

**Fix:**
Ensure you are using a valid Kubernetes version in the 'kind create cluster' command.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31, improved error messages during cluster creation.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME                 STATUS   ROLES    AGE   VERSION' with the nodes listed and their statuses as 'Ready'.
codeSnippets: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y docker.io

prerequisites.description: Ensure Docker is installed and running before proceeding with Kind installation.
